name: claude-chrome
description: Claude in Chrome - browser automation via the official Anthropic extension. Control your logged-in Chrome browser, automate workflows, fill forms, extract data, and run scheduled tasks.
capabilities: Navigate to URLs and manage multiple browser tabs
Click buttons, type text, and fill out web forms automatically
Extract structured data from web pages and save as CSV files
Access authenticated pages in Gmail, Google Docs, Notion, and CRMs
Monitor console logs and network requests for debugging
Record browser workflows as GIFs and run them on schedules
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation (SKILL.md) describing how to use the official Claude Chrome extension and Chrome DevTools MCP server. No executable code is present. All 96 static findings are false positives from pattern-matching on markdown documentation content, code block delimiters, and example prompts.
valueStatement: Manual browser tasks consume too much time. This skill enables Claude to control your Chrome browser, automating repetitive form filling, data extraction, and workflow tasks using your existing login sessions.
limitations: Requires Google Chrome specifically. Other Chromium browsers are not supported.
Requires a paid Claude plan such as Pro, Team, Enterprise, or Max.
Cannot operate in headless mode with the extension. DevTools MCP supports headless.
Does not work with WSL or mobile devices.

useCases.title: Automated Form and UI Testing
useCases.description: Test web forms with invalid data and verify error messages appear correctly. Check console for errors during page loads.

useCases.title: Extract Data from Authenticated Pages
useCases.description: Pull product listings, prices, and availability from web pages you are already logged into. Save results as CSV.

useCases.title: Automate Repetitive Data Entry
useCases.description: Fill CRM contact records or application forms automatically from CSV files. Schedule recurring tasks like inbox cleanup.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Navigation
promptTemplates.scenario: Visit a website and click an element
promptTemplates.prompt: Go to github.com/anthropics and click on the Code tab

promptTemplates.title: Form Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Open localhost:3000, try submitting the login form with invalid data, and check if error messages appear correctly

promptTemplates.title: Data Extraction
promptTemplates.scenario: Extract structured data from a page
promptTemplates.prompt: Go to the product listings page and extract the name, price, and availability for each item. Save as CSV.

promptTemplates.title: Authenticated Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Open my Google Analytics, get this week's traffic summary, and save it to a file

bestPractices: Use fresh tabs when pages become unresponsive to avoid getting stuck on errors
Be specific about elements and actions to produce consistent automation results
Add verification steps for long tasks to confirm all items were completed correctly
antiPatterns: Using browser automation for simple one-click tasks that are faster to do manually
Leaving CAPTCHAs or modal dialogs unhandled during automation runs
Running automation on dynamic pages without waiting for elements to fully load

faq.question: Which browsers are supported?
faq.answer: Only Google Chrome is supported. Brave, Arc, Edge, and other Chromium browsers do not work with this skill.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Claude plan?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires a Pro, Team, Enterprise, or Max Claude plan to function.

faq.question: Can I use this with other MCP servers?
faq.answer: Yes. Chrome DevTools MCP can run alongside other MCP servers in your Claude Code configuration.

faq.question: Is my browsing data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The extension uses Chrome Native Messaging API locally. Site permissions control access. High-risk actions require confirmation.

faq.question: Why is my browser not responding?
faq.answer: Check for blocking modal dialogs such as alerts or prompts. Dismiss them manually or restart the Chrome extension.

faq.question: How is this different from Puppeteer or Playwright?
faq.answer: This uses your real Chrome browser with existing login sessions. No re-authentication needed. More convenient but less isolated.
